---
pagetitle: "Ran Wang | Academic Website"
page-layout: full
toc: false
---

::: {.hero-name role="heading" aria-level="1"}
Ran Wang
:::

::: {.hero-kicker}
Economic Geography · Scientific Mobility · Regional Innovation
:::

::: {.hero-summary}
I am an empirical researcher in economic geography. My work examines how scientists move across institutions and cities, how knowledge production is organized across space, and how regional innovation systems are shaped by mobility, institutions, and city-level opportunities. I build longitudinal relational data from career histories, publications, institutions, and cities, and use network analysis, event-level destination-choice models, and spatial measurement to study scientific mobility.
:::

::: {.hero-actions}
[Research](research.html){.btn .btn-hero-primary}
[Publications](publications.html){.btn .btn-hero-secondary}
[Projects](projects.html){.btn .btn-hero-outline}
[Download CV](assets/cv/Ran_Wang_CV_Public.pdf){.btn .btn-hero-outline}
:::

::: {.photo-credit}
Photo: John Rodenn Castillo / Unsplash
:::

::: {.panel .panel-light}
## Current Research Focus

My current work focuses on elite biomedical scientists as a window into scientific mobility and the geography of knowledge production. One line of research models observed intercity moves as event-level destination choices. A broader line builds scientist-institution-city data to study mobility networks, collaboration records, and regional innovation conditions while keeping these concepts analytically distinct.
:::

::: {.panel .panel-muted}
## Selected Work

::: {.theme-grid}
::: {.theme-card}
### Pre-move Publication-Location Exposure and Destination Choice among Elite Biomedical Scientists

Working paper on whether observed destinations were already visible in scientists' origin-anchored pre-move publication-location environments.
:::

::: {.theme-card}
### Spatial Structure and Determinants of Top Global Biomedical Scientists' Mobility from an Urban Network Perspective

Accepted article in *World Regional Studies* on intercity mobility networks and factors associated with mobility-tie formation and intensity.
:::

::: {.theme-card}
### Scopus Research Data Toolkit

Public Python toolkit for reproducible Scopus-oriented workflows using synthetic fixtures, offline mock transport, and documented measurement boundaries.
:::
:::
:::

::: {.panel .panel-dark}
## Methods

Longitudinal relational data construction; scientist-institution-city linkage; entity resolution; social and spatial network analysis; ego-network analysis; conditional logit for event-level destination choice; WERGM/ERGM for network projects; Python, R, Stata, GIS, NetworkX, Gephi, and Ucinet.
:::

::: {.panel .panel-light}
## Quick Links

::: {.quick-links}
[Research](research.html)
[Publications](publications.html)
[Projects](projects.html)
[CV](cv.html)
[Contact](contact.html)
[GitHub](https://github.com/Olivia-wr-research)
:::
:::
